The Ancient Church; Peter
⍞ Materials: Redemption: The Church in Ancient Times

➜ INTRO
In the Bronze Bow, you read a historical fiction book about what was happening in Judea during the time of Jesus around 30 AD. This book tells true accounts of the Christians who carried on the message of Jesus after he ascended into heaven. The events in this book take place from around 30 AD to 350 AD. This was still a time when the Romans controlled all the areas around the Mediterranean Sea. All the people you will meet lived in Roman occupied territories.

➜ LOCATE
⍞ Map Resource: Historical Atlas of the World

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
1.p.11-12 "At not point" - "was increasingly marginalized." Then last paragraph on p. 13 "The story of.."

2.We will read about the apostle Peter. What do you remember about him? (He cut off the ear of a soldier when Jesus was arrested in the Garden of Gethsemane, he denied Jesus three times) The event we will read about is told in the Bible in Acts 2. This event is called the "Birthday of the Church." It happened 40 days after the resurrection and 10 days after Jesus ascended to heaven.
p. 17-21 "Peter took the" - "And prayed."

➜ PROMPT
∞ Image Link: Title of Image

• DEFINITIONS
frayed: fragile or uncertain (p.11)
ambassador: representative or messenger (p.11)
marginalized: given an unimportant or powerless position within a society or group (p.12)
zealot: member of a first-century Jewish political movement that fiercely opposed the Roman occupation of Judea (p.20)

Peter; begin Paul
⍞ Materials: Redemption: The Church in Ancient Times

➜RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ INTRO
You are reading parts of two chapters today. You will finish the chapter about Peter and find out what happened and why we call that special event "The Church's Birthday." Then you will start the section on Paul who was not one of the original twelve apostles of Jesus but was used mightily by God to spread the gospel message.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.21- 25 "Peter did not know"-end

2.Now we meet the apostle Paul whose story is also told in the Bible. He wrote 13 of the 27 books of the New Testament. His writings were initially letters or epistles written to various churches. The story we are reading today can be found in **Acts \_\_\_.**
p.29-31 "This way!" - "but little comfort."

➜ PROMPT
∞ Image Link: Many artists have painted scenes of the Holy Spirit coming at Pentecost. Take a moment to look at these two paintings.

• DEFINITIONS
gale: a strong current of air (p.21)

Shavuot: Jewish holiday (p.23)

lunacy: insanity, madness (p.29)

orator: (p.29)
